title: Yang 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
Yang 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Paper
Yang et al. 2024

title: Yang 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Setup
Packages: readxl, dplyr, ggplot2, ggrepel, yaml.

title: Yang 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Import data
Sheets imported: Cys Starv_Cys 0µM and Cys Starv_Cys 1µM.

title: Yang 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Filter to genes only
No row filtering applied.


Rows (0 µM): 18423 


Rows (1 µM): 18449

title: Yang 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Plots

Beta score scatter: 0 µM vs 1 µM
This plot replicates figure S1B from the original publication, confirming that the data was imported and processed correctly.
MAGeCK beta scores for each gene plotted across the two cysteine conditions (0 µM vs 1 µM). Genes with consistently negative beta scores in both conditions — appearing in the lower-left quadrant — are candidates whose loss is detrimental under cysteine limitation regardless of its severity. Highlighted genes (red) are those labelled by the original authors.







Volcano plot
Custom plot to inform the selection of thresholds for candidate gene extraction. This plot shows the 0 µM cysteine condition only, which is the sole condition represented in our internal list of CRISPR screens. Highlighted genes (red) are the same as those labelled in the beta score scatter above, as selected by the original authors. The dashed line marks p-value = 0.001, the threshold applied below. Dots stacked along the top edge of the plot represent genes with a p-value of exactly 0, i.e. –log10(0) = ∞.

title: Ferroptosis Data Extraction
text:
FerrDB v3: Used to extract known ferroptososis modulators and their roles.




Published CRISPR datasets are used to extract lists of candidate genes for meta-analysis.



Cross-checking extracted gene lists against FerrDB v3 for known ferroptosis modulators.

title: Ferroptosis Data Extraction
text:

title: FerrDb – Known Ferroptosis Modulators extraction
text:
Setup
Packages: readr, dplyr, ggplot2.

title: FerrDb – Known Ferroptosis Modulators extraction
text:
Load data
Exclude ferrdb_disease.csv (no gene information) and ferrdb_inducer.csv and ferrdb_inhibitor.csv (document substances, not genes). Filter each file to ferroptosis entries only via the RCD column.


Registered S3 method overwritten by 'bit64':
  method          from 
  print.bitstring tools

title: FerrDb – Known Ferroptosis Modulators extraction
text:
Bar plot
Each bar shows the number of distinct genes annotated with that role in FerrDB for ferroptosis. The count label is printed above each bar for easy reference.

title: FerrDb – Known Ferroptosis Modulators extraction
text:
Export


Wrote 1957 rows to data/ferrdb_genes.tsv

title: Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
text:
Paper
Timson et al. 2024

title: Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
text:
Setup
Packages: readxl, dplyr, ggplot2, ggrepel, yaml.

title: Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
text:
Import data
Sheet imported: Table S1 Gene Scores.

title: Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
text:
Filter to genes only
Remove rows whose Names entry starts with sg (guide RNA controls) or starts with INTERGENIC (non-gene features).


Rows before filtering: 23940 


Rows after filtering:  2997

title: Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
text:
Rank genes
Genes ranked by ascending score in each condition.

title: Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
text:
Plots

Cyto score ranked plot
This plot replicates figure 2D (left panel) from the original publication, confirming that the data was imported and processed correctly.
Genes ranked by ascending cytoplasmic gshF score under BSO treatment. GCLM is highlighted as a positive control — it encodes the modifier subunit of glutamate-cysteine ligase, a key enzyme in glutathione biosynthesis. The dashed lines mark the ±1.5 threshold used to extract the high- and low-score gene lists below.







Mito score ranked plot
This plot replicates figure 2D (right panel) from the original publication, confirming that the data was imported and processed correctly.
Same as above for the mitochondrial gshF condition. GCLM is again highlighted as a reference point.







Cyto rank vs mito rank (log-log)
This plot replicates figure 2C (left panel) from the original publication, confirming that the data was imported and processed correctly.
Cross-condition comparison of gene ranks on log scales. Genes in the lower-left corner score poorly (low rank) in both conditions, suggesting relevance to glutathione homeostasis regardless of compartment. Highlighted genes are known those labelled by the original authors in their figure, used here as reference points to confirm the data was imported and processed correctly.

title: Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
text:
Extract gene lists


$timson_2024_cyto_high_score
[1] 1

$timson_2024_cyto_low_score

[1] 1

title: Zhang 2022 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
Zhang 2022 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Paper
Zhang et al. 2022

title: Zhang 2022 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Setup
Packages: readxl, dplyr, ggplot2, ggrepel, yaml.

title: Zhang 2022 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Import data
Sheet imported: Supplementary Table 1.

title: Zhang 2022 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Filter to genes only
No row filtering applied.


Rows: 18054

title: Zhang 2022 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Plots

Volcano plot
This plot replicates figure 1a (right panel) from the original publication, confirming that the data was imported and processed correctly.
Volcano plot for the 72-hour Erastin treatment condition. Genes highlighted in red (PCBP1, GPX4, ACSL4, KEAP1, PKCβ) are those labelled by the original authors in their figure, used here as reference points to confirm the data was imported and processed correctly. The dashed line marks p-value = 0.001, the threshold applied below. Dots appearing at the top edge of the plot have p-values below 10^-6 (the upper limit of the y-axis); any with p = 0 represent numerical infinities (–log10(0) = ∞).
